titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Chester and Phebe Fuson Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 8, 2018 at 17:33

did.abstract: Letters, writings, collected material, and photos document religious work and political conditions in South China from 1905 to 1929. Chester Garfield and Phebe Meeker Fuson were American Presbyterian missionaries in South China from 1905 to 1929. Chester Fuson taught at Canton Christian College (later Lingnan University) from 1905 to 1917. Thereafter he was an administrator and educator for the South China Mission of the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.)

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Chester and Phebe Fuson Papers (RG 143), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Taught at Canton Christian College (later known as Lingnan University)

Accompanied American scientist on magnetic survey, traveling 5,500 miles from Peiping through Honan, Shensi, Kansu, Sinkiang, over the Kwenlun and Himalaya mountains to India. Elected a Fellow of the Royal Geographical Society for participation in this survey.
Married Phebe Meeker. Children: Ben, Nelson, William.

Appointed by Board of Foreign Missions of Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A. to serve its South China Mission. While in Canton, CGF was Executive Secretary of the Board of Primary Education of the provincial Christian Education Association and was on the faculty of Pui Ying Middle School as Director of Teacher Training.

Transferred to Linhsien (Linchow) where CGF aided in educational work, was in charge of country churches and was acting superintendent of the Linhsien Hospital from 1941 on. He was South China Mission Executive Secretary until furlough in 1944.
Returned to Linhsien following furlough; was active in relief and rehabilitation work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Fuson Papers provide valuable documentation of religious and political conditions in South China from 1905 to 1949. The family correspondence in Series I includes more than 200 letters from Chester and Phebe Fuson to their sons and other family members; it is most substantive for the years 1938 to 1940. General correspondence includes printed and typescript report letters, early letters to the Trustees of Canton Christian College (1906-1908) that are particularly interesting for their descriptions of College athletic activities, correspondence with members of the First Presbyterian Church of Pueblo, Colorado, which supported CGF as its foreign pastor, and miscellaneous other correspondence to or from the Fusons. Writings by the Fusons, collected material and memorabilia provide additional documentation of their life and work in South China.

list.item: III. Personal Items and Memorabilia, 1915-1965, n.d.

did.unittitle: Account of first trip into Canton city
Account of village clan fights near CCC campus
Narrative of 2nd South China track meet held in Canton including CCC and many other schools
Narrative of expedition from Canton to Linhsien
"The Fiftieth Anniversary Celebration Pui Ying Middle School"
"Personal Report of Mrs. Chester G. Fuson"
Farewell messages to Fusons on their retirement
Miscellaneous personal memoranda (incl. financial statement and instructions regarding burial)
Miscellaneous printed items that document the work of the Fusons
Ming Sum School for the Blind, Canton - report
Letters to family from Fusons, incl. written from Manila, Hong Kong
Letters to family from Fusons, written from Linchow and Linhsien
Letters to Fusons: from Chinese friend, farewell letters from church and hospital, and from a leper
Letters from Fusons to churches in U.S. and "Dear Friends"; circular letters of other missionaries, incl. from Florence Meeker Raetz, who was in charge of Door of Hope Mission, Canton
Writings by Chester Fuson: "A Trip to Loh Fau Mountain"; "A Modern Chinese Wedding"; "Three Weeks in Kwangtung and Kwangsi"
Writings by Fusons: "The Taai Cheung Ling Paai (Aborigine settlement)"; Station report; "A Trip to Macao"
"Literary Grandmothers" by E. Marshall; "Principal's Report" by Wan Kam Ming (Pui Ying Middle School in Hong Kong)
Photocopied selections from autograph book of Phebe Fuson, with explanatory notes by son William M. Fuson; program from memorial meeting for Mrs. Van Etten, Linchow

Photographs (loose) - incl. Canton Christian College staff, Presbyterian mission staff, Bible study group, Canton Christian College soccer team, Sunday School group photo, students, Christmas play, illustrations of Robert Morrison
Photographs (matted) - incl. first year high school class at Canton Christian College, faculty of Pui Ying School, Chinese teachers' wives, modern Chinese wedding
Photograph of mission staff and children on occasion of Machle retirement, with sheet providing identification of all
Oversize panoramic photo of students and staff of Union Normal School, Canton
